evals.prompt: Value this company: FCF next year 100M growing 10% for 5 years then 3% terminal; WACC 12%. Net debt 250M. 50M shares. What's the per-share value?
evals.expected_output: Explicit FCFs years 1-5 (100, 110, 121, 133.1, 146.41), TV at end of year 5 = 146.41x1.03/(0.12-0.03) = 1,675.5; discount all at 12% -> EV ~ 1,395M; equity = ~1,145M; ~22.9/share. Numbers verified by code; TV share of EV (~68%) noted.
assertions: Computes terminal value off year-5 FCF grown at 3%
Bridges EV to equity by subtracting net debt before dividing by shares
Runs the math in code and shows a sensitivity or TV-dependence note

evals.prompt: Our DCF says the company is worth 900M but EV/EBITDA comps suggest 1.4B. Management wants to average them to 1.15B. Advise.
evals.expected_output: Don't average a 35%+ divergence - diagnose it: WACC currency/inflation mismatch, overly conservative projections, peak-cycle comp multiples, non-comparable peers, IFRS 16 convention mismatch, or missing bridge items. Reconcile, then conclude a defended range.
assertions: Refuses to mechanically average divergent methods
Lists plausible diagnostic causes for the gap on both DCF and comps sides
Mentions the IFRS 16 or normalization consistency checks
Concludes with a process to reconcile rather than a split-the-difference number

evals.prompt: EV concluded at 2.0B. Balance sheet: debt 600M, cash 150M (of which 40M is restricted in a sanctioned jurisdiction), 30% minority interest in a sub with book value 80M and estimated fair value 200M, pension deficit 50M pre-tax (tax 30%), associate stake worth 90M. Get me to equity value.
evals.expected_output: Equity = 2,000 - 600 + 110 (usable cash; haircut/exclude the 40M restricted) - 200 (MI at fair value) - 35 (pension net of tax) + 90 (associate) = ~1,365M, with each judgment explained.
assertions: Haircuts or excludes the restricted cash with rationale
Uses minority interest at fair value (200M) not book (80M)
Takes the pension deficit net of tax (35M)
Arrives at approximately 1,365M with an itemized waterfall

evals.prompt: Run a DCF on the attached projections (evals/files/fcf_projections.csv): discount the five FCF years at the stated WACC, compute Gordon terminal value, enterprise value, and bridge to equity value using net debt.
assertions: Discounts terminal value back 5 years (does not leave it undiscounted)
